edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:b1e1d544af47
edges.original_sentence: Non-targeted oldﬁeld species thus appeared to be of sufﬁcient quality to support rodent populations (peaking at 23 individuals ha /C01 in summer), but without rescuing rarer prairie species from targeted granivory and resulting in reduced prairie diversity outside of experimental exclosures.

source_spans.text: Non-targeted oldﬁeld species thus appeared to be of sufﬁcient quality to support rodent populations (peaking at 23 individuals ha /C01 in summer), but without rescuing rarer prairie species from targeted granivory and resulting in reduced prairie diversity outside of experimental exclosures.

source_document.title: Selective plant foraging and the top‐down suppression of native diversity in a restored prairie

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:62ffb4153337
edges.original_sentence: These findings suggest that rodents may reduce the restoration success of tallgrass prairie in our region, with some planted species likely to suffer future recruitment difficulties due to granivory.

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:62ffb4153337
edges.original_sentence: The exclosed plots had twice the abundance of planted native seedlings ( F 1,5 = 5/C192, P = 0/C10196) and signiﬁcantly higher richness of planted species ( F1,5 = 13/C18, P < 0/C1001) than areas lacking protection from small mammals (Figs 2 and S1).

source_spans.text: The exclosed plots had twice the abundance of planted native seedlings ( F 1,5 = 5/C192, P = 0/C10196) and signiﬁcantly higher richness of planted species ( F1,5 = 13/C18, P < 0/C1001) than areas lacking protection from small mammals (Figs 2 and S1).

source_document.title: Selective plant foraging and the top‐down suppression of native diversity in a restored prairie

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:62ffb4153337
edges.original_sentence: The exclosed plots had twice the abundance of planted native seedlings ( F 1,5 = 5/C192, P = 0/C10196) and signiﬁcantly higher richness of planted species ( F1,5 = 13/C18, P < 0/C1001) than areas lacking protection from small mammals (Figs 2 and S1).

source_spans.text: The exclosed plots had twice the abundance of planted native seedlings ( F 1,5 = 5/C192, P = 0/C10196) and signiﬁcantly higher richness of planted species ( F1,5 = 13/C18, P < 0/C1001) than areas lacking protection from small mammals (Figs 2 and S1).

source_document.title: Selective plant foraging and the top‐down suppression of native diversity in a restored prairie

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:0cb78476f89a
edges.original_sentence: There were varying relationships between the density of the rodent community by month and the rodent diet as revealed by the DNA analysis (Fig. 4). Rodent density per month and numbers of oldﬁeld species consumed per month were positively correlated ( R 2 = 0/C1166, P = 0/C1041), while no relationship was detectable between rodent density and native plant consumption ( R 2 = 0/C10786, P = 0/C143).

source_spans.text: There were varying relationships between the density of the rodent community by month and the rodent diet as revealed by the DNA analysis (Fig. 4). Rodent density per month and numbers of oldﬁeld species consumed per month were positively correlated ( R 2 = 0/C1166, P = 0/C1041), while no relationship was detectable between rodent density and native plant consumption ( R 2 = 0/C10786, P = 0/C143).

source_document.title: Selective plant foraging and the top‐down suppression of native diversity in a restored prairie

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:b1e1d544af47
edges.original_sentence: However, this seems to be insufﬁcient to shelter rarer prairie species from targeted granivory, which in turn may hamper their persistence.
